original_hypothesis.title: Marketplace Freeze Permanently Locks Listed Vesting Allocations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The unlistVesting function uses the isFreeze modifier. If the admin freezes the marketplace during an emergency, users cannot unlist their tokens to recover them. Even the admin cannot unlist tokens on behalf of users because the modifier applies to all callers.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Emergency pause mechanisms should typically allow users to withdraw their funds. By blocking unlisting, the protocol traps user assets during critical operational incidents.
preconditions: Users have active listings on the marketplace

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Withdrawal/unlisting function is blocked by the emergency pause modifier
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires admin to freeze the marketplace

operational_questions: Was it intended to block unlisting during a freeze, or is this an oversight?
How can users recover their assets if the marketplace is permanently frozen?
validation_requirements: Attempt to call unlistVesting as a seller and as the admin

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin freezes the marketplace due to a suspected vulnerability
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Users attempt to withdraw their listed tokens to secure them
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Transactions revert, leaving the tokens trapped in the potentially vulnerable VestingManager

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Users cannot access their funds during emergencies
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium - admin must unfreeze the marketplace to allow withdrawals

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Failed unlistVesting transactions during a freeze period

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
